text: Good code is clean code, which means it is easy to read and understand. One of the best ways to achieve readability is by using clear naming conventions. Variable and function names should tell you exactly what they do, even without comments. In addition, it is important to break your code into small, focused functions. Each function should do one thing only. This makes the code easier to test and reuse. Finally, clean code improves maintainability. When you or other developers come back to the code months later, they can quickly understand and modify it without confusion. Writing clean code saves time and reduces mistakes.
